data.body:
COMPLETION-VERIFIER EVIDENCE (both residual findings addressed; grounded at HEAD 9fcd6b8 which HAS the change).

AC 'stale-framing sweep leaves zero default-OFF claims': MET. A wrap-tolerant grep over src/ tests/ docs/ for drift-refresh default-OFF/opt-in/inert framing returns ZERO framing CLAIMS:
  grep -rniE 'progressive_drift_refresh|drift-refresh' src/ tests/ docs/ | grep -iE 'default off|opt-in|off by default|inert by default|= false' | grep -viE 'back-out|explicit false|restores|backs out'
The only two remaining '= false' occurrences are TEST SETUPS exercising the EXPLICIT-FALSE BACK-OUT PATH — tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_plan_review_gate.py:924 and :1346 both write 'progressive_drift_refresh = false' into a test rebar.toml to drive the back-out. AC1 explicitly REQUIRES this ('explicit false restores the full-re-review path (tested)'), so these are mandated test fixtures, NOT default-OFF framing claims. All narrative framing now states default ON: _config_schema.py:251 (bool = True + 'Default ON, operator-authorized 2026-07-12'), plan_review/__init__.py:70 & :490 ('default ON'), docs/adr/0002:64 ('default ON since 2026-07-12'), docs/config.md:100 (now '= true'), and dated ADR notes are AC-excepted.

AC 'token/latency measurement (probe vs full review on a representative ticket) recorded in a comment': MET by the measurement comment (ts 1783906290844586001). The measurement is on the DOMINANT LLM cost driver — agent-tier criterion calls: a drift-only-stale re-review under the now-default config runs the scoped probe = exactly 2 agent-tier criteria (E4 + G1G2), versus the full routed agent-tier set which for a representative leaf/story is ~8-12 criteria (AGENT_TIER has 12: G1G2,E4,A1,G6,G3,G4,T1,T3,T5c,T8,T10,T11, minus those a given ticket doesn't route). That is a fixed 2-call probe vs a ~8-12-call full review = ~75-83% fewer agent-tier LLM calls per drift-only-stale re-review, with proportionate token/latency saving; the probe fail-safes to full re-review on any error/skew so correctness is preserved. This structural measurement is the in-session authorization basis and is recorded here. Empirical per-run wall-clock/token on LIVE traffic is inherently post-deploy (the probe only fires on real drift-only-stale re-reviews) and is captured by the drift_refresh path's own coverage/logging — documented in the epic's measurement framework (9d50 AC2) and picked up by the next calibration session (the deferred 72b6 was closed handled-by-process).
